---
title: "5.3.2 Keeping data safe from security threats"
subtitle: "Privacy settings and SSL · P209-P210"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .figure-placeholder { border: 1px dashed #8aa0bf; border-radius: 8px; background: #f4f8ff; color: #40526b; padding: 1.1em 1.3em; margin: 0.8em 0; font-size: 0.9em; text-align: center; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.68em; line-height: 1.06; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.2em 0.3em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)

## Privacy Settings

### Limiting access to personal profile

<span class="term">Privacy settings</span> (隐私设置) are controls available on web browsers, social networks and other websites.

They are designed to limit who can access and see a user's personal profile.

## Privacy Setting Examples

### Tracking, payments and browsing

Privacy settings can refer to:

::: {.tight-list}
- a "do not track" setting to stop websites collecting and using browsing data
- checking whether payment methods have been saved on websites
- safer browsing alerts when the browser encounters a potentially dangerous website
- web browser privacy options such as storing browsing history and cookies
:::

## Privacy Setting Examples

### Advertising and apps

Privacy settings can also refer to:

::: {.tight-list}
- website advertising opt-outs
- third-party tracking for advertising purposes
- app settings, for example switching off location data sharing in map apps
:::

## Secure Sockets Layer

### Protocol for secure communication

<span class="term">Secure Sockets Layer (SSL)</span> is a type of protocol: a set of rules used by computers to communicate with each other across a network.

This allows data to be sent and received securely over the internet.

## SSL and HTTPS

### Encrypted data

When a user logs onto a website, SSL encrypts the data.

Only the user's computer and the web server are able to make sense of what is being transmitted.

A user will know if SSL is being applied when they see <span class="mark">https</span> or the small padlock in the status bar.

## HTTP vs HTTPS Address

### Secure clue in the browser

::: {.clean-table}
| Protocol shown | Example |
|---|---|
| using https | `https://www.xxxx.org/documents` |
| using http | `http://www.yyyy.co.uk/documents` |
:::

## Figure 5.23

### Secure sockets layer process

::: {.figure-placeholder}
Figure 5.23 Secure sockets layer SSL screenshot placeholder
:::

## SSL Sequence

### Connection and certificate

::: {.tight-list}
- the user's browser sends a message so that it can connect with the SSL-secured website
- the browser requests that the web server identifies itself
- the web server responds by sending a copy of its SSL certificate to the browser
- if the browser can authenticate the certificate, it sends a message back to allow communication to begin
- the web server acknowledges the browser and SSL-encrypted two-way data transfer begins
:::

## SSL Certificate

### Authenticating a website

An <span class="term">SSL certificate</span> is a form of <span class="term">digital certificate</span> used to authenticate a website.

This means any communication or data exchange between browser and website is secure, provided this certificate can be authenticated.

## Where SSL Is Used

### Textbook examples 1/2

SSL would be used for:

::: {.tight-list}
- online banking and all online financial transactions
- online shopping/commerce
- sending software to a restricted list of users
- sending and receiving emails
- using cloud storage facilities
:::

## Where SSL Is Used

### Textbook examples 2/2

SSL would also be used for:

::: {.tight-list}
- intranets and extranets
- Voice over Internet Protocol when video chatting or audio chatting
- instant messaging
- social networking sites
:::

## Activity 5.6

### Textbook practice

The textbook asks students to:

::: {.tight-list}
- identify terms such as two-step verification, proxy server, privacy settings, SSL, firewall, DNS, biometrics, social engineering and pharming
- describe how SSL/TLS certificates are used for secure sharing of data
- identify clues in possible phishing emails
:::

## Classroom Check

### Privacy settings and SSL

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- privacy settings limit who can access and see a user's personal profile
- privacy controls include do not track, saved payment checks, safer browsing, browser history/cookies and location sharing
- SSL is a protocol for secure communication over a network
- SSL encrypts data so only the user's computer and web server can make sense of it
- https and the padlock indicate SSL may be applied
- an SSL certificate authenticates a website before secure data exchange
:::

## Bridge

### Unit 5 complete

This deck completes the formal Unit 5 knowledge content. P211 is extension material, and P212-P216 are summary, key terms and exam-style questions.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 5 Directory / 返回 Unit 5 目录](../../index.html)
